tweets.text:
From the Markets Update (https://t.co/9vUw5AcbqF):

Friday all about Tech.

Despite the SPX gain, sector breadth was one of the weakest of the month with just 2 of 11 sectors higher according to CME Cash Indices with just one up 1% or more, but again it was heavyweight Tech +1.9%, keeping the index afloat.

In contrast five sectors down around -1% more led by Staples -2% (and Comm Services was -1.7%). Energy down for the 6th day in 7.

tweets.text:
For the month, if we weren't coming off the best months since 2020 for the Nasdaq and SPX, it would have been the best since 2023 at +8.4% and +5.2% respectively. 

RUT was a robust +4.3%, and even the DJIA had a very strong month +2.8%. https://t.co/leV45fYyzC
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
For the week, just the DJIA saw negative territory at any point, while the Nasdaq, RUT, and SPX jumped at the open Tuesday and would add to that Thursday. 

The RUT would give up its lead Friday to finish +1.9% behind the Nasdaq's +2.5%. SPX was +1.4% while the DJIA was +0.9%. https://t.co/MwJlyURfD9
